claim_text: Naive keyword filtering was not reliable for identifying performance-related agentic pull requests in this corpus, so the study relied on LLM-based classification and manual recovery to construct the final performance dataset.
caveats: There is visible tension between the 92% false-positive rate from keyword filtering and the much lower 7.5% false-positive rate reported for the LLM-based review sample, which supports the shift in method rather than contradicting it.
mechanism_or_bounds: The supported mechanism is procedural rather than causal; keyword matches often captured pull requests where performance language was present without performance being an actual concern, but the evidence does not establish why those false positives were so common beyond that labeling mismatch.
contradiction_note: There is visible tension between the 92% false-positive rate from keyword filtering and the much lower 7.5% false-positive rate reported for the LLM-based review sample, which supports the shift in method rather than contradicting it.

claim_text: The final performance-related pull request set was distributed across many repositories, agents, and software-stack areas rather than concentrated in a narrow optimization niche.
caveats: No direct contradiction is visible, but the claim is bounded by topic-model quality and grouping choices rather than direct observation of all possible performance work.
mechanism_or_bounds: The evidence supports breadth of distribution only: 1,160 LLM-identified pull requests spanned 447 repositories and five agents, and topic modeling yielded 52 topics grouped into 10 higher-level categories, but the study does not show whether this spread reflects underlying demand, agent behavior, or dataset composition.
contradiction_note: No direct contradiction is visible, but the claim is bounded by topic-model quality and grouping choices rather than direct observation of all possible performance work.

claim_text: Performance-related pull requests were rejected more often than non-performance pull requests in the studied agentic development data.
caveats: No contradiction is visible in the reported comparison, but the result is descriptive and should not be read as proof of a single rejection mechanism.
mechanism_or_bounds: The evidence establishes the comparison in outcomes, not the cause; the paper does not show whether the higher rejection rate is driven by technical difficulty, review standards, risk sensitivity, or other repository-specific factors.
contradiction_note: No contradiction is visible in the reported comparison, but the result is descriptive and should not be read as proof of a single rejection mechanism.

claim_text: Among accepted performance-related pull requests, merge times varied by category, with UI, AI, and Analytics taking longest and Low-level work merging fastest, while change size showed only weak association with merge time.
caveats: There is mild tension with any simple larger-change explanation because the reported association between change size and merge time was weak, even where it was strongest.
mechanism_or_bounds: A bounded interpretation is justified: category-level differences appear more salient than raw change size in this dataset, but the evidence does not establish the causal mechanism behind slower merges for UI, AI, and Analytics work.
contradiction_note: There is mild tension with any simple larger-change explanation because the reported association between change size and merge time was weak, even where it was strongest.
